Sabad..personality: The Sabadilla patient is a chilly, hypersensitive individual who craves warmth in all forms—hot drinks, hot air, warm wrappings. They are deeply susceptible to environmental stimuli, particularly odors, and even the mere thought of certain triggers can provoke physical symptoms. There is a marked tendency toward nervousness, hysteria, and an unbalanced mental state accompanied by various nervous manifestations.
Sabad..mental_state: The mind is dominated by erroneous impressions and fixed delusions about the body—imagining limbs are crooked, the chin elongated, the body withering or shrunken, or believing oneself pregnant from mere flatus. Mental exertion aggravates symptoms and paradoxically induces sleepiness; the patient falls asleep while meditating or reading. Thinking itself worsens complaints, and there is a stupefying quality to the headaches and mental fog, particularly in feeble, overtaxed children.
Sabad..emotional_pattern: The emotional life is colored by groundless fears and imaginary suffering—the patient believes in horrible diseases and fatal outcomes with no objective basis, yet the distress is more intense than if real pathology existed. There is a hysterical quality with convulsive tremblings and cataleptic episodes, and the patient often receives no sympathy from others because the suffering appears unfounded. Appetite and desire fluctuate between complete aversion and sudden hunger once eating begins, reflecting the same pattern of emotional instability between disgust and engagement.
